raw:
{
  "judge_id": "responsibility-bearing judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.5": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the framing problem) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.5": "The agent proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, including reordering of sections to improve clarity.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for several claims, but does not provide confidence levels or missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free and identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., contradictory contribution claims).",
    "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced, but minor edits are not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.5": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the framing problem) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.5: The agent proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, including reordering of sections to improve clarity.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for several claims, but does not provide confidence levels or missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free and identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., contradictory contribution claims).
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced, but minor edits are not annotated with source and reason.
